ddls:
CREATE TABLE "ip_blocks" (
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each IP address block */,
  "cidr_notation" TEXT /* CIDR notation of the IP address block */,
  "address_range_start" TEXT /* Starting address of the range */,
  "address_range_end" TEXT /* Ending address of the range */,
  "number_of_addresses" TEXT /* Total number of addresses in the block */,
  "scope" TEXT /* Scope of the IP address block (e.g., software, private network, host, subnet) */,
  "purpose" TEXT /* Purpose of the IP address block */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the block was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the block was last updated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the IP block */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the IP block is active or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("block_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ip_blocks_purpose FOREIGN KEY ("purpose") REFERENCES purposes ("purpose_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ip_blocks_scope FOREIGN KEY ("scope") REFERENCES scopes ("scope_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "ip_addresses" (
  "ip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each IP address */,
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the IP address block */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* The specific IP address */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the IP address (e.g., available, assigned, reserved) */,
  "assigned_to" TEXT /* Device or user to which the IP address is assigned */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the IP address was assigned */,
  "deassigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the IP address was deassigned */,
  "mac_address" TEXT /* MAC address of the device using the IP address */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device using the IP address (e.g., server, desktop, laptop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ip_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ip_addresses_block_id FOREIGN KEY ("block_id") REFERENCES ip_blocks ("block_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "scopes" (
  "scope_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each scope */,
  "scope_name" TEXT /* Name of the scope (e.g., software, private network, host, subnet) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the scope */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("scope_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "purposes" (
  "purpose_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purpose */,
  "purpose_name" TEXT /* Name of the purpose (e.g., broadcast, local communication, loopback) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the purpose */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("purpose_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., network admin, IT manager, security officer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activity_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the activity */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., assign, deassign, reserve) */,
  "ip_id" INTEGER /* ID of the IP address involved */,
  "block_id" INTEGER /* ID of the IP address block involved */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the activity */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_block_id FOREIGN KEY ("block_id") REFERENCES ip_blocks ("block_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_ip_id FOREIGN KEY ("ip_id") REFERENCES ip_addresses ("ip_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subnet_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subnet assignment */,
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the IP address block */,
  "subnet_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the subnet */,
  "subnet_cidr" TEXT /* CIDR notation of the subnet */,
  "subnet_range_start" TEXT /* Starting address of the subnet range */,
  "subnet_range_end" TEXT /* Ending address of the subnet range */,
  "subnet_description" TEXT /* Description of the subnet */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the subnet was assigned */,
  "assigned_by" INTEGER /* User who assigned the subnet */,
  "vlan_id" INTEGER /* VLAN ID associated with the subnet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subnet_assignments_assigned_by FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subnet_assignments_block_id FOREIGN KEY ("block_id") REFERENCES ip_blocks ("block_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subnet_details" (
  "subnet_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the subnet */,
  "subnet_cidr" TEXT /* CIDR notation of the subnet */,
  "subnet_range_start" TEXT /* Starting address of the subnet range */,
  "subnet_range_end" TEXT /* Ending address of the subnet range */,
  "gateway" TEXT /* Gateway address for the subnet */,
  "dns_servers" TEXT /* DNS servers for the subnet */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the subnet */,
  "subnet_mask" TEXT /* Subnet mask for the subnet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subnet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dhcp_servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the DHCP server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the DHCP server */,
  "server_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the DHCP server */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the DHCP server */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dhcp_server_ip_blocks" (
  "association_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the association */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the DHCP server */,
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the IP block */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("association_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dhcp_server_ip_blocks_block_id FOREIGN KEY ("block_id") REFERENCES ip_blocks ("block_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dhcp_server_ip_blocks_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES dhcp_servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ip_address_audit" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the audit log */,
  "ip_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the IP address */,
  "previous_status" TEXT /* Previous status of the IP address */,
  "new_status" TEXT /* New status of the IP address */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the change */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ip_address_audit_ip_id FOREIGN KEY ("ip_id") REFERENCES ip_addresses ("ip_id")
)
